action.reasoning: Observation: Custom per-target pose matching with relative azimuth implemented and validated on initial val objects (5 objs succeeded with correct RGBA outputs), but full eval crashed after ~40-50 objs (last successful uid 46459ea37d6244abacd42cd2480bbc6). LPIPS remains at 0.6121 from non-aligned orbit baseline. Debug command revealed sorted uids and sample cameras (elev varies significantly e.g. input -15.5 to target -79). Analysis: Custom get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth matches inspected TripoSR source but can produce NaNs for certain az/elev (cross(lookat,up)=0 -> normalize failure); per-obj renderer calls + no cache may accumulate mem. Pose alignment (rel_az=(target-input)%360, per-target elev/dist) should fix mismatch vs fixed orbit starting at ~0 (dataset has large relative az ~185° avg), yielding major LPIPS drop. TripoSR zero-shot remains optimal (task-aligned, <1B params, no training). Decision: Update predict.py with robust normalizations (eps=1e-8 in F.normalize, torch.pi, device-consistent focal), add per-view/obj debug prints + torch.cuda.empty_cache()/gc.collect() to prevent OOM/crash, test on 60 val objs covering crash point. If succeeds submit_eval immediately for new metric + time meas. before considering training.
action.command:
python -c '
from pathlib import Path
data_path = Path("/data/val")
object_dirs = [d for d in data_path.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and (d / "input.png").exists()]
object_dirs = sorted(object_dirs)
uids = [d.name for d in object_dirs]
print("Total objects:", len(uids))
if "46459ea37d6244abacd42cd2480bbc6" in uids:
    idx = uids.index("46459ea37d6244abacd42cd2480bbc6")
    print("Index of last successful:", idx, "uid:", uids[idx])
    if idx + 1 < len(uids):
        print("Next uid to test:", uids[idx+1])
        print("Next few:", uids[idx+1:idx+5])
    else:
        print("Was last")
else:
    print("Uid not found in list")
print("\nSample first uids:", uids[:3])
' && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/val_predictions.json --num_objects=60
